description: Phase 2 Alpha 1 PRDs for common-stack happy path coverage beyond the completed MVP walking skeleton. Stories follow the 7-PRD breakdown in `spec/alpha-1/prd-alpha-1-{01..07}-*.md`; each story has a corresponding US-XXX section in its PRD with full TDD acceptance criteria. Source of truth: `spec/ROADMAP.md` Phase 2 and complete spec parts in `spec/{01..19}-*.md`.

userStories.title: Automate provider-lando setup on Linux
userStories.description: As a Linux alpha tester, I can run `lando setup` and get the managed runtime bundle downloaded, verified, and recorded without manually wiring sockets. (PRD-01 US-001)
acceptanceCriteria: Failing test covers `meta:setup` invoking provider-lando setup with a fake bundle downloader and fake Podman API client
Checksum verification follows §5.8.1 and fails closed with a tagged remediation error
Setup stores provider state in the configured cache/state directory, not process memory
Compiled `$bunfs` path and source OCLIF path produce equivalent setup output
`lando setup` and `lando shellenv` derive `LANDO_INSTALL_DIR` from `process.execPath` in the compiled binary per §17.1 stage 7; tests assert agreement between the two commands
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Add provider-lando macOS machine lifecycle
userStories.description: As a macOS alpha tester, I can create/start/stop/teardown the managed Podman machine through provider-lando. (PRD-01 US-002)
acceptanceCriteria: Fake-client unit tests cover create, start, stop, upgrade, and teardown transitions
ProviderCapabilities declares macOS support with `bindMountPerformance: "slow"`
`lando setup` reports actionable remediation when host virtualization prerequisites are missing
Live test is gated behind an explicit macOS provider env var and skipped by default
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Complete provider-lando capability matrix
userStories.description: As the planner, I can choose provider-lando based on a complete capability matrix instead of MVP assumptions. (PRD-01 US-003)
acceptanceCriteria: Schema test asserts every Alpha 1 capability field has a provider-lando value for Linux and macOS
Planner rejects unsupported Alpha 1 features with a tagged error before apply

Capability output is surfaced by `meta:doctor` diagnostics: every `ProviderCapabilities` field from §5.4 appears in the selected-provider check, and missing-capability failures include service, feature, capability, provider id, and suggested fix per §5.4
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Make provider-docker feature-complete on Linux and macOS
userStories.description: As a user with Docker Desktop or system Docker, I can use provider-docker as an Alpha 1 alternative path. (PRD-01 US-004)
acceptanceCriteria: Docker Engine HTTP API fake-client tests cover apply, inspect, exec, logs, and destroy
Linux socket and macOS Docker Desktop paths are supported through config/env discovery
Provider declares `bindMountPerformance: "slow"` for Docker Desktop or VM-mediated paths and `"native"` for Linux native Docker sockets per §5.4
Live Docker tests are gated by `LANDO_TEST_DOCKER_SOCKET` or `DOCKER_HOST`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Persist cross-process provider state needed for lifecycle commands
userStories.description: As a user, I can run `start`, `info`, `stop`, and `destroy` from separate CLI processes without relying on in-memory provider caches. (PRD-01 US-005)
acceptanceCriteria: Scenario test starts an app, launches a fresh runtime, then runs info/stop/destroy using persisted plan/state
Destroy receives the planned `AppPlan` and preserves app-scoped volumes unless explicitly requested
Provider-specific transient state is stored under the provider state directory with cache encoding rules from §12.2
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Harden provider cleanup and failure recovery
userStories.description: As an alpha tester, failed start/stop operations leave containers, networks, and volumes in documented states. (PRD-01 US-006)
acceptanceCriteria: Fake provider tests cover partial apply failure after network creation and after one service start
Cleanup removes app-scoped containers/networks and preserves volumes by default
Errors include remediation plus `providerId`, operation name, redacted details, and original cause fields required by §5.7 so manual cleanup identifies the affected provider operation
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Expose provider diagnostics in `meta:doctor`
userStories.description: As a tester filing bugs, I can run `lando doctor` and include provider/runtime diagnostics. (PRD-01 US-007)
acceptanceCriteria: `meta:doctor` reports selected provider, version, socket/machine status, the §5.4 capability summary, and §10.9 solution records with `automatic` or `manual` remediation
Doctor never requires app bootstrap unless app-specific diagnostics are requested

JSON renderer output is covered by the named snapshot fixture `meta-doctor.provider-status.ndjson`, asserting event order, provider fields, capability fields, severity, context, and solution records per §10.9
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Keep provider contract suite authoritative
userStories.description: As a provider author, I can run one contract suite against TestRuntimeProvider, provider-lando, and provider-docker. (PRD-01 US-008)
acceptanceCriteria: `@lando/sdk/test` exposes the provider contract suite drafted in MVP with Alpha 1 lifecycle coverage
TestRuntimeProvider and provider-docker pass in default CI
provider-lando live cases remain env-gated where host runtime is required
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Ship PHP service types for common frameworks
userStories.description: As a PHP user, I can define `php:8.2` or `php:8.3` with Drupal, WordPress, Laravel, Symfony, or no framework. (PRD-02 US-009)
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceType tests cover normalized plans for php versions and framework options
Generated service plans include app mounts from §6.4, `LANDO_*` env from §6.9, healthcheck intent from §6.7, and framework web server hints from §6.12.2
Unsupported PHP versions fail during planning with remediation
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Ship Node service types beyond MVP
userStories.description: As a Node user, I can use `node:lts` or `node:22` in real app plans. (PRD-02 US-010)
acceptanceCriteria: Node ServiceType tests cover both versions and app-root bind mounts
Plans work with providerExec tooling as a default execution target
Existing MVP Node+Postgres scenario remains green
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Ship Python service types for Django/FastAPI/Flask
userStories.description: As a Python user, I can define Python 3.12 apps with supported framework hints. (PRD-02 US-011)
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceType tests cover django, fastapi, flask, and none framework options
Plans expose default endpoint ports from §6.6 and command metadata from the selected framework preset in §6.12.2
Unsupported framework values fail schema validation
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Ship Ruby service types for Rails and generic Ruby
userStories.description: As a Ruby user, I can define Ruby 3.3 services for Rails or generic workloads. (PRD-02 US-012)
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceType tests cover rails and none framework options
Rails default command/port metadata is represented as provider-neutral intent
Planner output uses provider-neutral `ServicePlan` fields from §6.2 and §6.10, with provider-specific fields limited to `providerInfo` or `providers.<id>` extensions per §5.6
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Ship common database/cache/web services
userStories.description: As a team, we can compose apps with mysql, mariadb, postgres, redis, nginx, apache, static, and raw Compose passthrough. (PRD-02 US-013)
acceptanceCriteria: Each listed service has schema and ServiceType plan tests

Raw Compose passthrough accepts `image:` and Compose `build:` for `type: compose` per §6.12.1; fields without provider-neutral semantics must live under `providers.<id>` and are marked non-portable per §5.6. Tests exercise both `provider-lando` and `provider-docker` codepaths
Compose-declared volumes follow the same destroy-preserves-volumes rule as Lando-managed volumes (per provider-lando lifecycle contract)
Database services declare storage scope defaults and credentials/env contract
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Support Alpha 1 mount and storage semantics
userStories.description: As a user, I can use app-root bind mounts, bind/volume mounts, excludes, and app/service scoped storage. (PRD-02 US-014)
acceptanceCriteria: Landofile parser tests cover `mounts:` bind and volume entries plus `excludes:` patterns
Planner maps excludes to volume-shadow behavior only; Mutagen/file sync is not implemented
Storage `scope: global` is rejected until the global app phase

Landofile parser rejects Alpha-3-only sections (`includes:` per §7.7, configuration expressions per §7.3.1, `secrets:` per §4.2 SecretStore + §7.4 top-level key, env overrides per §7.6) with a tagged NotImplemented + Alpha 3 remediation; one test per rejected section
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Extend LandofileService to load `landofile.ts`
userStories.description: As an advanced recipe author, I can author a programmatic Landofile (`landofile.ts`) and have `LandofileService` execute it deterministically. Unblocks PRD-04 US-029. (PRD-02 US-014b)
acceptanceCriteria: `LandofileService` loads `landofile.ts` files via Bun's TS loader and returns a parsed Landofile equivalent to the YAML form; YAML and TS forms produce identical `AppPlan` output
Programmatic execution is sandboxed: no remote module fetch, no host shell-out, no filesystem access outside the app root; violations fail with a tagged `LandofileSandboxError` + remediation
Execution timeout is bounded and configurable; default timeout fails with `LandofileTimeoutError`
Schema validation runs on the returned object; failures surface the same `LandofileParseError` shape as the YAML path
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Out-of-sequence story in PRD-02 (priorities 9-16); priority 28.5 places the landofile.ts loader before the PRD-04 US-029 story it unblocks.

userStories.title: Emit the basic `LANDO_*` environment contract
userStories.description: As code running inside services, I can rely on basic app/service/host path environment variables. (PRD-02 US-015)
acceptanceCriteria: Service plan tests assert app id, service id, and §6.9 host path env vars for every §6.12.1 catalog family shipped in Alpha 1
Provider exec tests verify env reaches command execution
Env var names are documented in the PRD/source docs touched by the implementation
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Plan per-app networking and provider-exec healthchecks
userStories.description: As an app user, services share a per-app bridge and basic healthchecks can gate readiness. (PRD-02 US-016)
acceptanceCriteria: Planner tests assert per-app network intent for multi-service apps
Healthcheck support is limited to provider-exec mode and rejects unsupported modes clearly
`lando start` readiness output uses inspected provider state, not blind success
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Parse Alpha 1 `tooling:` Landofile section
userStories.description: As a user, I can define basic tooling commands in `.lando.yml`. (PRD-03 US-017)
acceptanceCriteria: Landofile parser tests cover `tooling:` tasks with `cmds:`, `service:`, `description:`, and Alpha 1 `vars:` forms: literal value, `default:`, `sh:`, and `prompt:` per §8.5.3; remote vars and unsafe `raw:` interpolation are deferred
Unsupported Alpha-3-only tooling features fail with remediation, not silent ignore
Parsed tooling contributes to command registry at bootstrap `tooling`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Implement providerExec ToolingEngine
userStories.description: As a user, default tooling runs inside the selected service through the runtime provider. (PRD-03 US-018)
acceptanceCriteria: ToolingEngine unit tests verify command execution delegates to `RuntimeProvider.exec`
Service resolution selects the declared `service:` value or the primary app service from the planned `ServiceInfo` set per §6.10; tests cover the no-primary error path
Exit code/stdout/stderr are preserved through CLI rendering
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement host ToolingEngine
userStories.description: As a user, I can opt into host-mode tooling backed by Bun.$. (PRD-03 US-019)
acceptanceCriteria: Host engine tests cover success, non-zero exit, cwd, and env propagation
Shell errors map to tagged ShellExecError/remediation patterns from existing services
Host mode is available for `lando shell` in Alpha 1; service shell remains Alpha 3
Host engine is the runtime for `.bun.sh` script-backed tasks (§8.5.9) and `vars.<name>.sh:` evaluation (§8.5.3); the §8.5.9 realpath-containment rule applies and is regression-tested
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Compile tooling command registry on cold path
userStories.description: As the CLI, I can discover tooling commands during bootstrap and cache the resulting command index. (PRD-03 US-020)
acceptanceCriteria: Compilation pipeline tests cover Landofile tooling to command specs
Cold path writes plugin/tooling command index cache using §12 encoding
Hot-path optimization is explicitly not required in Alpha 1
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Support `.bun.sh` script-backed tasks
userStories.description: As a power user, I can define script-backed tasks without a separate plugin. (PRD-03 US-021)
acceptanceCriteria: Parser and execution tests cover `.bun.sh` task discovery described by §8.5.9
Scripts run with documented cwd/env and failure behavior
Script-backed tasks cannot escape declared host/provider execution mode without explicit config
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement `lando exec`, provider-exec `lando ssh`, and host-mode `lando shell`
userStories.description: As a user, I can execute commands, use Alpha 1 `ssh` as provider-exec TTY command behavior, and open host-mode shells from the CLI. (PRD-03 US-022)
acceptanceCriteria: Scenario tests cover `exec` against providerExec with fake provider output
`ssh` behavior is documented as provider-exec TTY command behavior only; SSH sidecar/subsystem work remains deferred
`shell` host mode works; service shell and SSH subsystem/sidecar features return NotImplemented/Alpha 3 remediation

Compiled and source CLI paths match for supported commands: identical exit code, stdout, stderr tag/remediation structure, and JSON renderer event fields after normalizing timestamps and temp paths per §8.2 and §17.1 stage 7
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Fill Alpha 1 app command coverage
userStories.description: As an app user, most `app:*` commands work except explicitly deferred commands. (PRD-03 US-023)
acceptanceCriteria: Scenario tests cover the §8.2 command subset `app:start`, `app:stop`, `app:restart`, `app:rebuild`, `app:destroy`, `app:info`, `app:logs`, `app:config`, and `app:cache`
Deferred `app:includes:*` and `app:config:translate` return structured NotImplemented remediation
Compiled `$bunfs` handlers mirror source command behavior until full OCLIF dispatch lands
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Fill Alpha 1 meta/app management commands
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I can run app list/poweroff and basic plugin/config/setup/doctor/bun/x commands. (PRD-03 US-024)
acceptanceCriteria: Scenario tests cover `apps:list`, `apps:poweroff`, `meta:config`, `meta:plugin:add` npm source, `meta:plugin:remove`, `meta:setup`, `meta:doctor`, `meta:bun`, and `meta:x`
`meta:plugin:add` npm source validates manifest/module containment before loading any plugin code
`meta:plugin:add` warns/confirms that plugins run as trusted host code while trust/signing is deferred; non-interactive mode requires an explicit trust/confirm flag

Trust decisions are in-memory for the current install only — no persistent trust store in Alpha 1; a persistent trust store is deferred to Alpha 3 and a test asserts the prompt fires with a fresh `<userConfRoot>` per test invocation
Deferred plugin trust/new/test/build/link/unlink/publish commands return structured remediation
Command aliases follow the top-level alias conflict rules
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Parse full Alpha 1 `recipe.yml` schema
userStories.description: As a recipe author, I can write `recipe.yml` using the Alpha 1-supported schema. (PRD-04 US-025)
acceptanceCriteria: Parser tests cover metadata, prompts, files, Landofile output, and postInit sections from §8.8
Schema rejects Alpha-3-only remote/dynamic fields with remediation
MVP hardcoded recipe path migrates to the recipe parser without breaking MVP scenario
Tests pass

userStories.title: Support built-in prompt types

acceptanceCriteria: CLI fixture tests cover each prompt type with deterministic stdin/stdout
Secret prompts do not echo values in renderer transcripts
Prompt validation errors are recoverable in interactive mode and typed in non-interactive mode

userStories.title: Ship cwd-only recipe source

Recipe resolution is deterministic and does not perform network access

Bundled recipe files are embedded in the compiled binary via `scripts/build-bundled-recipes.ts` → `core/src/recipes/bundled.ts` with no disk read at runtime; codegen drift is caught by the bundled-codegen gate in PRD-07 US-049
Tests pass

userStories.title: Ship canonical common-stack recipes
userStories.description: As an alpha tester, I can scaffold the canonical v4.0 recipe set from §8.8.10, including WordPress, Laravel, node-api, Django, FastAPI, and Rails apps. (PRD-04 US-028)
acceptanceCriteria: Each §8.8.10 canonical recipe has a scenario test that runs init, parses the generated Landofile, and verifies the recipe snapshot names the generated service ids, `type:` entries, and tooling command ids
The binary ships the full §8.8.10 canonical recipe set in Alpha 1; Drupal and v3-style recipe compatibility shims remain out of scope per §8.8.10
Recipe READMEs document alpha limitations and required host prerequisites
Tests pass

Lint passes

userStories.title: Support programmatic `landofile.ts` output
userStories.description: As an advanced recipe author, I can generate a Landofile programmatically during init. (PRD-04 US-029)
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover a recipe that emits Landofile data via `landofile.ts` and validates the result through LandofileService
Programmatic output is sandboxed to documented inputs; no remote code fetch is allowed

userStories.title: Implement Alpha 1 `postInit` bun install action
userStories.description: As a recipe user, post-init can run Bun install where the recipe declares it. (PRD-04 US-030)
acceptanceCriteria: Scenario test covers `postInit: bun: { verb: install }` using a fake process runner or fixture package
`bun install` runs only inside the generated destination/app directory, never global Lando/plugin/cache directories
Tests reject outside-destination `cwd` or path traversal attempts for postInit actions
Docs/remediation explicitly state network access and package lifecycle script behavior for `bun install`
Unsupported postInit actions (`script`, `add`, `create`, `run`, `x`) return Alpha 3 remediation
Post-init failures leave clear instructions, redact env/registry token values in output, and do not delete generated files
Tests pass

Lint passes

acceptanceCriteria: CLI tests cover renderer flag parsing and config/env precedence

userStories.title: Implement concurrent task tree events
userStories.description: As a user, long-running operations show task tree progress for concurrent work. (PRD-05 US-034)
acceptanceCriteria: Renderer tests cover `task.tree.start`, `task.start`, `task.complete`, and `task.fail` events

Concurrent task ordering is covered by the named snapshot fixture `renderer.task-tree.concurrent.ndjson`, asserting `task.tree.start`, child `task.start`, `task.detail`, terminal event, and `task.tree.complete` order per §8.9.2
Plain renderer remains readable in non-TTY CI logs

Renderer subscriber stream is materialized before the first event publish per the EventService no-replay contract; cold-start regression test asserts no events are dropped between bootstrap and the first `task.tree.start`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Implement message info/warn/error contract

acceptanceCriteria: Unit tests cover `message.info`, `message.warn`, and `message.error` in plain/json/lando renderers
Warnings do not change exit code unless command fails separately

userStories.description: As a user, the lando renderer paints a useful first banner before long-running work. (PRD-05 US-036)
acceptanceCriteria: TTY fixture test covers `paint.banner` first-paint behavior from §8.9.1: a single-line pre-bootstrap banner on stdout, emitted before plugin import, within the 50 ms cold first-byte budget, and handed off once to the Renderer Layer
Full first-paint contract and expand/collapse remain marked Beta 1/deferred
Non-TTY output avoids control sequences

acceptanceCriteria: Fixture lists every deferred command surface for Alpha 1: `apps:scratch:*`, `meta:global:*`, `app:includes:*`, `app:config:translate`, `meta:plugin:trust*`, and `meta:plugin:{new,test,build,link,unlink,publish}`. A snapshot test asserts each command returns a tagged NotImplementedError with phase-specific remediation
Tests assert every not-yet-shipped command returns NotImplementedError with target phase remediation
Remediation text uses the roadmap phase names from `spec/ROADMAP.md` Phase 2 and §14 open-decision labels, and does not include stack traces or source file paths
Compiled `$bunfs` fallback mirrors source OCLIF guard behavior
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement persistent cwd-app-map cache
userStories.description: As the CLI, I can resolve known apps across processes using a durable cwd-app map. (PRD-06 US-041)
acceptanceCriteria: CacheService tests cover write/read/delete/list for cwd-app-map entries

userStories.title: Implement persistent app plan cache
userStories.description: As the planner, I can reuse app plans across CLI invocations when inputs have not changed. (PRD-06 US-042)
acceptanceCriteria: Plan cache tests cover key derivation from Landofile/config/plugin/service inputs
Changing relevant input invalidates the cached plan

Cache writes use `CacheService.writeAtomic` true atomic rename semantics (write-temp-then-rename) per §12; the rename adapter lives outside `FileSystem` and uses `node:fs/promises.rename` because Bun 1.2.18 has no atomic rename primitive on `Bun.file`; regression test asserts the temp file is renamed (not copied) into place
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

Cache is safe for missing bundled plugins and reports tagged errors

Old/unknown cache versions are ignored or migrated according to documented policy

userStories.title: Add schema and bundled-codegen snapshot gates to CI
userStories.description: As maintainers, schema changes and bundled-plugin/recipe set changes are intentional and visible in PR review. (PRD-07 US-049)
acceptanceCriteria: CI includes a schema-snapshot diff job generated by `scripts/build-ci-workflow.ts`
Schema-snapshot scope is `@lando/sdk` schema exports plus the bundled plugins' manifest schemas; out-of-tree plugin manifests are not part of the gate

CI re-runs `scripts/build-bundled-plugins.ts` and `scripts/build-bundled-recipes.ts` and fails via `git diff --exit-code` when `core/src/plugins/bundled.ts` or `core/src/recipes/bundled.ts` is out of date
Failing test covers generator output for both the schema gate and the bundled-codegen gate
Schema-snapshot and bundled-codegen update commands are documented (`bun run codegen`)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Add library API and recipe test layers to CI
userStories.description: As maintainers, Alpha 1-only surfaces are continuously verified. (PRD-07 US-050)
acceptanceCriteria: Generated CI workflow runs library API tests and recipe test layer separately or as named steps
Workflow tests assert the job/step names so branch protection can target them
Env-gated live provider tests remain skipped unless secrets/host config exist
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Promote Linux x64 workflow artifact to dev prerelease
userStories.description: As an alpha tester, I can download a Linux x64 binary from the dev channel. (PRD-07 US-051)
acceptanceCriteria: Release workflow promotes successful build artifact to a GitHub prerelease tagged for dev/alpha
Dev prerelease publishes a single `SHA256SUMS` text file alongside the binaries (one line per artifact, `<sha256>  <filename>` format); no GPG/cosign signing required in Alpha 1
No signing, SBOM, installer scripts, or self-update are required in Alpha 1, but `SHA256SUMS` publication is required
Promotion is generated from scripts/codegen, not hand-edited YAML

Sandboxed first-launch test runs the distributed artifact in a network namespace with egress blocked and asserts exit 0 plus no non-loopback `connect()` calls under `strace -e trace=network` (no telemetry beacon) — enforces the index non-goal
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Publish `@lando/core` alpha package to npm dev tag
userStories.description: As an embedding tester, I can install `@lando/core@4.0.0-alpha.N` from npm dev tag. (PRD-07 US-052)
acceptanceCriteria: Package publish workflow for `@lando/core` uses workspace build artifacts and dry-run test coverage

The packaging plan documents whether workspace dependencies are bundled, published as dev-tag dependencies, or otherwise resolved without changing the roadmap's named `@lando/core` Alpha 1 distribution scope
Publishing uses protected CI credentials or trusted publishing; no local developer token path is accepted
Publish command/config uses `--tag dev` and has a test/assertion that `latest` is not changed
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

Docs state Linux x64 binary + npm `dev` install (Linux/macOS) support and Windows deferral clearly; macOS users use the npm path until macOS binary promotion lands in Alpha 3
Docs are linked from release notes or README
